fact_verdicts.fact: 1. Action id is gov_action1mr0qdz2jmagvsch6r08fhqvq6vu8jakt4c8m9s7ea7z0p740vntqq4yjd6j; action type is TreasuryWithdrawals; proposed epoch is 639 and expires after 646.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 0 exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: 2. Anchor title is "Se7en Labs: Daedalus Wallet Maintenance and Improvements 2026-2027".
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the anchor document title.

fact_verdicts.fact: 3. The on-chain reward entry requests 1785333000000 lovelace to stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v; the budget table total is 1,785,333 ADA.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor onChain rewards entry and budget table state these values.

fact_verdicts.fact: 4. The proposal describes Daedalus as Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet, running an embedded Cardano node and deriving wallet and governance data directly from chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the abstract.

fact_verdicts.fact: 5. The proposal states opt-in telemetry shows approximately 4,000 monthly active users and says the true count is meaningfully higher because privacy-conscious full-node users are less likely to opt in.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the abstract.

fact_verdicts.fact: 6. The proposed work covers three areas in the abstract: Protocol Maintenance; Ecosystem Expansion; and User Support.
fact_verdicts.note: The abstract lists these three areas.

fact_verdicts.fact: 7. The proposal states Protocol Maintenance includes node upgrades and hard fork readiness for Leios, Peras, and Nested Transactions, with a compatible release at least 2 weeks before every mainnet hard fork.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated under Protocol Maintenance in the abstract.

fact_verdicts.fact: 8. The proposal states Ecosystem Expansion includes Keystone and Flex hardware wallet support, CIP-30 dApp connector, and Japanese localisation.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated under Ecosystem Expansion in the abstract.

fact_verdicts.fact: 9. The proposal states User Support includes responsive support for Daedalus users, dedicated attention to the Japanese community, and keeping Daedalus fully translated for Japanese users.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated under User Support in the abstract.

fact_verdicts.fact: 10. The proposal states Daedalus is free and open-source under Apache License 2.0 and that Se7en Labs does not monetize Daedalus usage or capture customer relationships, subscription revenue, token value, or IP exclusivity from this work.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in Nature of This Proposal.

fact_verdicts.fact: 11. The proposal states funded outputs include source code, build tooling, documentation, and release artifacts as public assets under Apache 2.0.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in Nature of This Proposal.

fact_verdicts.fact: 12. The proposal says the team assumed responsibility for Daedalus under an IOG contract in January 2026 and has shipped Mithril Snapshot Bootstrap, UTxO-HD / LSM Backend, Apple Silicon builds, the drt Release CLI, and Nix Build Modernization since then.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in Daedalus Delivery Record.

fact_verdicts.fact: 13. The proposal lists in-progress work as DRep Selection Dashboard, Ouroboros Genesis support, hardware wallet support for newer devices, Electron upgrade, and partial Mithril sync.
fact_verdicts.note: These items are listed as in progress.

fact_verdicts.fact: 14. The proposal states Se7en Labs has operated under a direct IOG contract for Daedalus maintenance since January 2026, closing August 31, 2026.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in Proven Capabilities and Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure.

fact_verdicts.fact: 15. The proposal states Se7en Labs has not received ada from the Cardano Treasury within the last 24 months.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in Proven Capabilities and Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure.

fact_verdicts.fact: 16. The proposal scope includes node and wallet backend currency, security and dependency maintenance, hard fork integration, Leios/Peras/Nested Transactions readiness, binary signing continuity, platform and localisation, hardware wallet support, CIP-30 dApp connector, user-facing CIP implementations, user support, and architecture assessment.
fact_verdicts.note: All listed items appear as Scope of Work headings or scope entries.

fact_verdicts.fact: 17. The Risk Register lists ADA/USD exchange rate volatility as High likelihood and High impact, mitigated by prompt conversion to USD-backed stablecoins and USD-denominated labor costs invoiced at spot rate.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Risk Register row.

fact_verdicts.fact: 18. The Risk Register lists cardano-node upstream API changes as Medium likelihood and High impact, mitigated by time-and-materials engagement and established working relationship with upstream IOG engineers.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Risk Register row.

fact_verdicts.fact: 19. The Risk Register lists Leios/Peras complexity underestimate as Medium likelihood and Medium impact, mitigated by time-and-materials removing fixed-price risk.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Risk Register row.

fact_verdicts.fact: 20. The Risk Register lists platform dependency changes as Low likelihood and High impact, mitigated by maintaining IOG binary signing and using the drt toolchain.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Risk Register row.

fact_verdicts.fact: 21. The Risk Register lists team continuity over 12-month contract as Low likelihood and High impact, mitigated by founding-member continuity and documented processes in drt and Nix build infrastructure.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Risk Register row.

fact_verdicts.fact: 22. Success metrics include compatible releases at least 2 weeks before each mainnet hard fork, cardano-node no more than 2 major versions behind mainnet recommended, 100% signed official releases, all four platform builds passing CI in every stable release, timely critical security fixes, Keystone and Flex support, CIP-30 stable release, maintained support including Japanese responses, full Japanese translation, and architecture assessment by Q3 2027.
fact_verdicts.note: All listed metrics appear in the Success Metrics section.

fact_verdicts.fact: 23. Milestones include Hard Fork Integration duration 6 weeks, Ongoing Maintenance duration 52 weeks, Leios/Peras Readiness duration 26 weeks, Hardware Wallet Support duration 12 weeks, CIP-30 dApp Connector duration 20 weeks, User Support duration 52 weeks, and Architecture Assessment duration 8 weeks.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the milestone headings and durations.

fact_verdicts.fact: 24. Budget summary line items are: Team 1,666,667 ADA; Test Hardware 33,333 ADA; Financial Audit 33,333 ADA; Intersect Budget Administration fee 52,000 ADA; Total 1,785,333 ADA.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Budget Summary table.

fact_verdicts.fact: 25. The budget text states any unspent labor, hardware, and audit budget is returned to the treasury.
fact_verdicts.note: The Team, Test Hardware, and Financial Audit budget bullets each state unspent budget is returned to the treasury.

fact_verdicts.fact: 26. The proposal says audit and oversight costs are included within overhead, the Intersect administration fee covers administrative oversight, and independent oversight will be provided through Intersect and technically capable third-party, including reporting obligations and milestone-based disbursement controls.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in Audit & Oversight.

fact_verdicts.fact: 27. The proposal says Intersect serves as administrator and independent milestone verifier, monitors use of funds, confirms acceptance criteria, and authorizes disbursements.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in Administration and Oversight.

fact_verdicts.fact: 28. The proposal says funds are disbursed monthly against verified work and held by Intersect in a dedicated account auditable by the Cardano Community before monthly disbursement to Se7en Labs.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in Administration and Oversight.

fact_verdicts.fact: 29. The proposal says Intersect will use the treasury management smart contract framework developed by Sundae Labs, with a 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract and one Project-Specific Smart Contract.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in Intersect Budget Management Tooling and Specifics.

fact_verdicts.fact: 30. The proposal names the Oversight Committee as Sundae Labs, Cardano Foundation, Dquadrant, NMKR, Sundial, and Eternl.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the named Oversight Committee members.

fact_verdicts.fact: 31. On-chain recipient history says stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v appears in 14 withdrawal proposals totalling 57273260000000 lovelace, first 2026-06-23 16:14:33 and last 2026-06-26 13:51:52.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 1.

fact_verdicts.fact: 32. On-chain DRep vote counts so far are yes=40, no=6, abstain=1, explicit ballots and not stake-weighted.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 1 gives yes=41, no=6, abstain=1, but the instructions say differing or absent moving ballot counts are not_found, not contradicted.

fact_verdicts.fact: 33. Treasury flow over 36 epochs is inflow 158838297854652 lovelace, enacted withdrawals 361435336000000 lovelace, ratio 2.275492.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 1.

fact_verdicts.fact: 34. BEACN doctrine prioritizes treasury stewardship and downside protection above evidence quality and reproducibility, public-benefit ecosystem growth, and execution speed/social consensus momentum, while constitutional integrity and protocol safety rank first.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Values Hierarchy ordering.

fact_verdicts.fact: 35. BEACN doctrine rejects first-come-first-served budget models and adopts a rolling-window Net Change Limit framework.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in Treasury Spending Doctrine Core Position.

fact_verdicts.fact: 36. BEACN doctrine states that for TreasuryWithdrawals without complete dossier, the current soft gate scores directionally with an explicit -0.10 caution penalty; incomplete diligence is recorded as explicit uncertainty.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Dossier Gate Posture soft gate section.

fact_verdicts.fact: 37. BEACN doctrine's AI cost axiom says requested ADA per deliverable should decrease where work can be substantially accelerated by AI tooling, and budgets should reflect post-AI costs rather than legacy labor models.
fact_verdicts.note: Directly supported by the Foundational Axiom section.

verification.summary: All checked facts are supported except fact 32, which is treated as not_found under the supplied instruction for moving ballot-count snapshots. No material discrepancies were found.
